questions.q: What weapon is Parashurama most famous for wielding?
questions.explanation: Parashurama's very name means 'Rama with the axe,' the parashu (axe) being his signature weapon.

questions.q: Parashurama is regarded as an avatar of which deity?
questions.explanation: Parashurama is counted among the ten principal avatars (dashavatara) of Vishnu.

questions.q: By caste or birth, what was Parashurama?
questions.explanation: Parashurama was born a brahmin, the son of the sage Jamadagni, yet lived as a warrior.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, Parashurama appears just after which dramatic event?
questions.explanation: Parashurama confronts Rama on the road shortly after Rama snapped Shiva's bow to win Sita.

questions.q: What is the common English description used for Parashurama on his card?
questions.explanation: His card subtitle calls him 'The Axe-Wielding Sage,' reflecting his role as a fierce brahmin-warrior.

questions.q: When Parashurama challenged Rama, what did he hand to Rama?
questions.explanation: Parashurama gave Rama his own mighty bow (the bow of Vishnu) and challenged him to string it.

questions.q: How did the encounter between Rama and Parashurama ultimately end?
questions.explanation: Rama effortlessly drew Parashurama's bow, and the elder sage recognized his power and yielded.

questions.q: Whose son was Parashurama?
questions.explanation: Parashurama was the son of the great sage Jamadagni and Renuka.

questions.q: Which Rama strung Shiva's bow that drew Parashurama out to confront him?
questions.explanation: It was Rama, prince of Ayodhya and son of Dasharatha, whose breaking of Shiva's bow brought Parashurama.

questions.q: Parashurama is traditionally said to be free from what, making him a deathless figure?
questions.explanation: Parashurama is counted among the chiranjivis, the immortals believed to still live across the ages.

questions.q: Who was Parashurama's mother?
questions.explanation: Parashurama's mother was Renuka, wife of the sage Jamadagni.

questions.q: From which deity did Parashurama obtain his famous axe through penance?
questions.explanation: Parashurama performed severe penance to Shiva, who granted him the parashu (axe) and martial knowledge.

questions.q: Parashurama is renowned in tradition for waging war repeatedly against which class?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Parashurama rid the earth of corrupt kshatriya kings many times over.

questions.q: Which king's killing of Jamadagni triggered Parashurama's vengeance, per tradition?
questions.explanation: The sons of King Kartavirya Arjuna slew Jamadagni, igniting Parashurama's war on the kshatriyas.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, on what occasion does Parashurama intercept Rama and his party?
options: On the journey back to Ayodhya after the wedding
At the start of the Lanka war
questions.explanation: Parashurama blocks the wedding party returning from Mithila to Ayodhya after Rama married Sita.

questions.q: What was the bow Parashurama gave Rama said to belong to?
questions.explanation: The bow Parashurama carried was the great Vaishnava bow, the bow of Vishnu, counterpart to Shiva's bow.

questions.q: After Rama drew the Vishnu bow, Parashurama asked Rama to do what with the nocked arrow?
options: Destroy his power to ascend or his accumulated merit
Aim it at the sky harmlessly
questions.explanation: Since the drawn arrow could not be wasted, Parashurama bade Rama strike down his power of motion (his ascetic merit).

questions.q: Which elder accompanying Rama tried to calm and appease the furious Parashurama?
questions.explanation: King Dasharatha, terrified, pleaded with Parashurama to spare his sons during the confrontation.

questions.q: How did Lakshmana behave toward Parashurama during the confrontation?
options: He shot an arrow at him
questions.explanation: Lakshmana, ever quick-tempered, scoffed at Parashurama's boasts and angered him further.

questions.q: After yielding to Rama, where did Parashurama withdraw to?
questions.explanation: Parashurama retired to Mahendra mountain to resume his austerities after recognizing Rama's divinity.

questions.q: The confrontation with Parashurama symbolically represents what transition?
options: From the old warrior-sage age to Rama's new age
From war to a second war
questions.explanation: As the card notes, the elder yielded to the new age, marking Rama's ascendancy as the era's hero.

questions.q: What sacred thread duty did Parashurama uniquely combine that made him unusual?
questions.explanation: Parashurama was a brahmin by birth yet lived as a fierce warrior, blending priestly and martial roles.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, the bow Parashurama carries is said to rival which other divine bow?
questions.explanation: The Vishnu bow Parashurama bears is described as the twin and equal of the Shiva bow Rama had just shattered.

questions.q: According to tradition, how many times did Parashurama clear the earth of unrighteous kshatriyas?
questions.explanation: Tradition states Parashurama rid the earth of corrupt kshatriyas twenty-one times over.

questions.q: Whose celestial cow did Kartavirya Arjuna seize, sparking the feud in Parashurama's backstory?
questions.explanation: Kartavirya Arjuna coveted and stole the wish-granting cow of Jamadagni, leading to the deadly feud.

questions.q: In which kanda (book) of Valmiki's Ramayana does the Parashurama episode occur?
questions.explanation: The encounter with Parashurama closes the Bala Kanda, the first book of the Ramayana.

questions.q: Parashurama explains that the spirit of which great Vishnu-portion had already entered Rama, making him yield?
options: His own ascetic energy was absorbed by Rama's
Rama held a piece of Shiva
questions.explanation: Recognizing Rama drew the imperishable Vishnu bow, Parashurama saw his own power pass into Rama and submitted.

questions.q: By father's lineage, Parashurama belonged to which sage's clan?
questions.explanation: Jamadagni was a descendant of the sage Bhrigu, making Parashurama a Bhargava (of the Bhrigu line).

questions.q: Which act of obedience did Parashurama perform at his father Jamadagni's command, per tradition?
questions.explanation: At Jamadagni's order Parashurama beheaded Renuka, and his unwavering obedience later won her revival as a boon.

questions.q: As a chiranjivi, Parashurama is traditionally said to reappear as a teacher of warriors in which later epic age?
options: He vanished after the Ramayana entirely
As a martial guru in the Mahabharata era
As a king of the gods
questions.explanation: Being immortal, Parashurama is said to live on and tutor warriors such as Bhishma, Drona, and Karna in the Mahabharata age.
